tasks.description: Add result-typed compilation function `tryCompileEffectAsync()` to `src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx`. Create a `CompileResult` discriminated union type (`{ ok: true; component: FC<EffectComponentProps> } | { ok: false; error: string }`). Implement `tryCompileWithTransform()` by extracting the try/catch from `compileWithTransform()` (lines 37-78) — on success return `{ ok: true, component }`, on catch return `{ ok: false, error: message }`. Export `tryCompileEffectAsync()` as the async variant (mirrors `compileEffectAsync`). Keep existing `compileEffect()` and `compileEffectAsync()` unchanged — they can internally delegate to the new function and unwrap. Validate with a quick manual test: call with valid code (expect ok:true) and invalid code (expect ok:false with error string).
tasks.executor_notes: Added `CompileResult` plus `tryCompileWithTransform()` / `tryCompileEffectAsync()` on the existing Sucrase pipeline. Manual verification showed valid code returns `{ ok: true }`, invalid code returns `{ ok: false, error }`, and `compileEffectAsync()` still unwraps to the fallback component behavior. TypeScript check passed.
commands_run: node --import tsx/esm --eval "import { tryCompileEffectAsync, compileEffectAsync } from './src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx'; const valid = \"function FadeEffect(props){ return React.createElement(AbsoluteFill, { style: { opacity: 1 } }, props.children); }\\nexports.default = FadeEffect;\"; const invalid = \"function Broken( {\\nexports.default = Broken;\"; const run = async () => { const validResult = await tryCompileEffectAsync(valid); const invalidResult = await tryCompileEffectAsync(invalid); const wrappedInvalid = await compileEffectAsync(invalid); console.log(JSON.stringify({ validOk: validResult.ok, validType: validResult.ok ? typeof validResult.component : null, invalidOk: invalidResult.ok, invalidError: invalidResult.ok ? null : invalidResult.error, wrappedInvalidType: typeof wrappedInvalid }, null, 2)); }; run();"

tasks.description: Define effect resource type and metadata interface in `src/features/resources/hooks/useResources.ts`. (1) Add `EffectMetadata` interface with fields: `name: string`, `slug: string`, `code: string`, `category: 'entrance' | 'exit' | 'continuous'`, `description: string`, `created_by: { is_you: boolean; username?: string }`, `is_public: boolean`. (2) Extend `ResourceType` union (line 78) to include `'effect'`. (3) Add `EffectMetadata` to the `ResourceMetadata` union (line 79). (4) Update `isResourceType()` guard (lines 94-96) to accept `'effect'`.
tasks.executor_notes: Defined `EffectMetadata` and extended `ResourceType`, `ResourceMetadata`, and `isResourceType()` to include `'effect'`. Verified all required type/guard sites were updated and TypeScript still passes.

tasks.description: Create effect resource hooks in new file `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectResources.ts`. (1) `useEffectResources(userId)` — wraps `useListResources('effect')` and `useListPublicResources('effect')` from useResources.ts, deduplicates by resource ID, returns effects grouped by category: `{ entrance: EffectResource[], exit: EffectResource[], continuous: EffectResource[] }`. Each entry includes the resource `id` (used as registry key) and all metadata fields. (2) `useCreateEffectResource()` — wraps `useCreateResource` with type='effect'. (3) `useUpdateEffectResource()` — wraps `useUpdateResource`. (4) `useDeleteEffectResource()` — wraps `useDeleteResource`. (5) Ensure query invalidation on mutations covers both user and public effect lists (match pattern from existing resource hooks).
tasks.executor_notes: `useEffectResources()` and the fixed-type create/update/delete wrappers are implemented and verified. Focused hook tests confirmed de-duplication by resource ID, correct grouping into `entrance`/`exit`/`continuous`, and correct mutation payload wrapping with `type: 'effect'`. TypeScript check passed.

tasks.description: Update `useEffectRegistry` (src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts) to dual-read from both effects table and resources table. Change signature to accept two sources: `legacyEffects: Array<{ slug: string; code: string }> | undefined` and `resourceEffects: Array<{ id: string; code: string }> | undefined`. Register legacy effects by slug (preserving `custom:{slug}` clip references). Register resource effects by resource ID (new `custom:{resourceId}` references). Update `VideoEditorProvider.tsx` (line 18-22) to also call `useEffectResources(userId)` and pass both sources. Add a code comment at the dual-read site explaining the legacy dependency and that cleanup is deferred to a future migration pass.

Verify DynamicEffectRegistry works with UUID-format keys. In `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts`, confirm that `normalizeName()` (lines 51-53) correctly strips `custom:` prefix from UUID strings. Confirm `get()` (lines 27-30) resolves UUID keys from the dynamic map. No code changes expected — this is a verification-only step. If any issues are found, fix them.

Create effect prompt templates in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts`. (1) System prompt specifying the Remotion effect component contract: EffectComponentProps interface, available globals (React, useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, interpolate, spring, AbsoluteFill), output format (exports.default = ComponentName), no import/export statements, use React.createElement not JSX. (2) Category-specific guidance: entrance effects animate in during `effectFrames`, exit effects animate out during final `effectFrames`, continuous effects animate across full `durationInFrames`. (3) Edit mode support: include existing code with user's change description. (4) Code extraction logic: strip markdown fences, validate structure. Base the prompt on the existing `effectPromptTemplate.ts` (37 lines) but expand with category-specific instructions.
tasks.executor_notes: Created `ai-generate-effect/templates.ts` with the effect component contract, category-specific guidance, edit-mode inclusion of existing code, and fenced-response extraction/validation helpers. Verified the prompt includes `React.createElement`, `exports.default`, category guidance, and embedded existing code in edit mode. TypeScript check passed.

tasks.description: Create the `ai-generate-effect` edge function in `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/index.ts`. Follow the `ai-prompt` pattern (supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts): (1) Use `bootstrapEdgeHandler` with auth required (JWT user auth). (2) Accept JSON body: `{ prompt: string, category: 'entrance' | 'exit' | 'continuous', existingCode?: string }`. (3) Rate limit per user using existing `_shared/rateLimit.ts` infrastructure. (4) Call Groq with the effect generation prompt from templates.ts, using `groqWithFallback` pattern. (5) Extract code from LLM response (strip markdown fences). (6) Return `{ code: string, model: string }`. (7) Deploy via `npx supabase functions deploy ai-generate-effect --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc`. (8) Test with curl for each category.
commands_run: npx supabase functions deploy ai-generate-effect --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc

tasks.description: Build the LLM-powered effect creator panel in `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx`. Dialog/sheet component with: (1) Category selector (entrance/exit/continuous) — required before prompting. (2) Natural language prompt textarea. (3) 'Generate' button calling `/functions/v1/ai-generate-effect` with loading spinner. (4) Code display (monospace textarea, matching CustomEffectEditor pattern) — editable for hand-tweaking. (5) Compilation status using `tryCompileEffectAsync()` from T1: green indicator + save enabled on success, red indicator + error message + save disabled on failure, 'Retry' button. (6) Edit mode: when opened with existing effect code, additional textarea for describing changes, submits both existing code and change description. (7) Name and description fields (auto-populated from prompt). (8) 'Save' button using `useCreateEffectResource()` / `useUpdateEffectResource()` from T3. (9) Public/private toggle defaulting to private. (10) Auto-compile on code change (debounced ~500ms).

Add inline preview for generated effects. (1) Create `EffectPreview` component (can be in the same file as EffectCreatorPanel or a sibling) that renders the compiled effect component wrapping a colored gradient rectangle. (2) Use the Remotion `Player` component to show a ~3-second loop at the composition's fps. (3) Wire into `EffectCreatorPanel` — preview appears below the code when compilation succeeds, disappears on failure.

Integrate the effect creator into ClipPanel (`src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx`). (1) Replace the 'Custom effects' section (lines 162-187) with a 'Create Effect' button that opens `EffectCreatorPanel` as a dialog. (2) When an existing custom effect is selected in a dropdown and it's a resource-based effect (UUID-format key after stripping `custom:` prefix), show an 'Edit' button that opens `EffectCreatorPanel` pre-populated with that effect's code and metadata. (3) Remove the old `CustomEffectEditor` import.

Show custom effects in the effect dropdowns in ClipPanel (lines 119-161). (1) Consume grouped effects from `useEffectResources` (T3's return value). (2) Update the entrance `<Select>` (lines 124-133) to append resource-based entrance effects after built-ins, separated by a disabled divider labeled '— Custom —'. (3) Do the same for exit and continuous `<Select>` components. (4) Each custom effect `<SelectItem>` uses `custom:{resourceId}` as its value and displays metadata `name` as label. (5) If resourceEffects are still loading, omit the custom section (built-ins always available).

Update BulkClipPanel and cleanup. (1) In `BulkClipPanel.tsx` (line 9, lines 328-359): replace `CustomEffectEditor` usage. Either integrate the new `EffectCreatorPanel` dialog trigger (preferred) or remove the custom effect section from bulk mode with a TODO comment. The key constraint: `CustomEffectEditor.tsx` CANNOT be deleted until BulkClipPanel no longer imports it — verify the build compiles. (2) Remove `CustomEffectEditor.tsx` and its imports from all files. (3) Remove `effectPromptTemplate.ts` (replaced by edge function templates). (4) Verify existing `custom:{slug}` clips from the effects table still render (dual-read path from T4). (5) Run TypeScript compilation check to ensure no broken imports.

End-to-end verification. (1) Test full flow: select category → describe effect → generate → preview → save → see in dropdown → apply to clip → render in Remotion player. (2) Test each category (entrance, exit, continuous). (3) Test public/private visibility. (4) Verify existing `custom:{slug}` clips still render. (5) Verify compilation errors show inline with retry and save gating. (6) Verify edit mode (open existing effect, describe changes, regenerate).

watch_items: FLAG-properties-panel-coverage: BulkClipPanel (line 9, lines 328-359) imports CustomEffectEditor. MUST update BulkClipPanel BEFORE deleting CustomEffectEditor.tsx or the build breaks. T12 handles this explicitly.

FLAG-legacy-effect-edit-path: Removing CustomEffectEditor makes effects-table entries read-only. This is an accepted tradeoff — legacy effects still render via dual-read, but can no longer be edited. Document this limitation.
DEBT-bulk-properties-panel: Same as FLAG-properties-panel-coverage — tracked in debt registry. Do not make worse.
DEBT-legacy-effect-lifecycle: Same as FLAG-legacy-effect-edit-path — tracked in debt registry. Do not make worse.

Identity scheme change: New effects use `custom:{resourceId}` (UUID). Legacy effects keep `custom:{slug}`. Both coexist in the registry. Never mix them — new code should only produce ID-based references.
Effects update in-place (no versioning): Editing an effect's code immediately affects all clips using it. This matches current behavior but is worth noting for testing.

Groq model suitability: The plan uses Groq (matching ai-prompt pattern) but Groq models may not be ideal for code generation. Monitor output quality and consider swapping to a better code model if generation quality is poor.
Rate limiting: The edge function must rate limit per user. Reuse existing _shared/rateLimit.ts infrastructure — do not create a new rate limit system.

Registry bootstrap timing: useEffectRegistry must finish registering before effects appear in dropdowns. If resourceEffects are loading, omit custom section from dropdowns (no loading spinner needed).
Sucrase compile pipeline: The tryCompileEffectAsync() function must use the same Sucrase pipeline as the existing compileEffect. Do not introduce a different compilation approach.
Public/private default: New effects default to private. The toggle to make public must be explicit.
Edge function deployment: Deploy individually with `npx supabase functions deploy ai-generate-effect --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc`. Never use bulk deploy.

sense_checks.question: Does `tryCompileEffectAsync()` correctly return `{ ok: false, error }` for invalid code AND `{ ok: true, component }` for valid code? Are the existing `compileEffect`/`compileEffectAsync` exports unchanged in behavior?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified `tryCompileEffectAsync()` returns `{ ok: true, component }` for valid code and `{ ok: false, error }` for invalid code. Existing `compileEffect()` / `compileEffectAsync()` behavior remains unchanged aside from delegating through the new result-typed path.

sense_checks.question: Is `'effect'` added to both the `ResourceType` union AND the `ResourceMetadata` union AND the `isResourceType()` guard? All three must be updated or queries will silently fail.
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `'effect'` was added to the `ResourceType` union, the `ResourceMetadata` union, and the `isResourceType()` guard.

sense_checks.question: Does `useEffectResources` correctly deduplicate effects that appear in both user and public lists (same resource ID)? Are effects grouped by category correctly?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified `useEffectResources()` de-duplicates identical resource IDs across private and public lists and groups the merged results into `entrance`, `exit`, and `continuous` buckets.

sense_checks.question: Does the dual-read path register BOTH legacy slug-based effects AND resource ID-based effects without key collisions? Does a clip using `custom:fade-up` (old) still resolve? Does a clip using `custom:a1b2c3d4-...` (new) also resolve?
Does `normalizeName('custom:a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890')` return the bare UUID? Does the registry's `get()` method resolve it from the dynamic map?
Does the system prompt specify React.createElement (not JSX), exports.default pattern, and category-specific animation guidance? Does edit mode include existing code in the prompt?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the template system prompt specifies `React.createElement`, the `exports.default = ComponentName` pattern, category-specific animation guidance, and edit mode includes the existing code in the prompt.

sense_checks.question: Does the edge function require auth, rate limit, and return clean code (markdown fences stripped)? Has it been deployed and tested with curl for all three categories?
sense_checks.executor_note: The edge function requires auth, enforces rate limiting, and returns cleaned code with markdown fences stripped; those behaviors are covered by unit tests. It has not been deployed or curl-tested here because the environment lacks a Supabase CLI access token, outbound DNS/network access, and Docker for local serving.

sense_checks.question: Does the panel gate saving on successful compilation (ok: true)? Does it show the error string inline on failure? Does the category selector prevent prompting without a selection?
Does the Remotion Player preview show the effect animating on a sample element? Does it disappear when compilation fails and reappear on success?
Is the old CustomEffectEditor import removed from ClipPanel? Does the 'Edit' button correctly detect resource-based effects (UUID format) vs legacy slug-based effects?
Do custom effects appear in the CORRECT category dropdown (entrance effects in entrance dropdown, etc.)? Is the value `custom:{resourceId}` (not slug)?
Does `npx tsc --noEmit` pass after removing CustomEffectEditor.tsx? Has BulkClipPanel been updated BEFORE the deletion? Is effectPromptTemplate.ts also removed?
Has the full flow been tested for all three categories? Do legacy `custom:{slug}` clips still render? Do compilation errors show inline with retry?

meta_commentary:
## Execution Guidance

**Parallelization:** T1, T2, and T6 have no dependencies and can be executed in parallel. T7 depends only on T6. T3 depends on T2. This gives good early parallelism.

**Critical ordering constraint:** T12 (cleanup) MUST update BulkClipPanel BEFORE deleting CustomEffectEditor.tsx. The build will break otherwise. This is the #1 gotcha in the plan.

**The two open flags are accepted tradeoffs, not blockers:**
- FLAG-properties-panel-coverage → handled by T12 explicitly updating BulkClipPanel first.
- FLAG-legacy-effect-edit-path → legacy effects become read-only. This is acceptable since the dual-read keeps them renderable and a future migration pass will move them to resources.

**Identity scheme is the most subtle change:** The switch from `custom:{slug}` to `custom:{resourceId}` affects how effects are stored on clips, looked up in the registry, and displayed in dropdowns. New code must consistently use resource IDs. Legacy slug-based references must continue to work through the dual-read path. Never accidentally register a resource-based effect by slug or a legacy effect by ID.

**Edge function gotchas:** (1) The Groq SDK and groqWithFallback pattern must be copied from ai-prompt, not reimplemented. (2) The _shared imports use Deno-style imports — match the existing function's import style. (3) Rate limit config needs a new entry for this function name.

**UI component sizing:** The EffectCreatorPanel is large (category selector + prompt + code editor + compilation status + preview + name/description + save). Use a Sheet or Dialog at a generous width. Match existing patterns from the codebase for dialog components.

**Compile pipeline subtlety:** The existing compileWithTransform (lines 37-78) catches errors at two levels: Sucrase transpilation AND Function constructor evaluation. The new tryCompileWithTransform must preserve both catch points and surface whichever error occurs first.

**Dropdown integration note:** The effect type arrays (entranceEffectTypes, exitEffectTypes, continuousEffectTypes from effects/index.tsx lines 68-70) are the built-in lists. Custom effects append AFTER these, not mixed in. Use a visual divider.

**BulkClipPanel decision:** The simplest approach for T12 is to replace the CustomEffectEditor usage in BulkClipPanel with a button that opens the same EffectCreatorPanel dialog. If that's too complex for bulk mode, a simpler fallback is to just remove the custom effect section from BulkClipPanel with a TODO comment — bulk custom effect editing was already a niche feature.
